runs.label: kras_g12d_apo_raf
runs.description: KRAS G12D (apo) + RAF RBD — baseline interaction

runs.label: kras_g12d_holo_raf
runs.description: KRAS G12D (MRTX1133-bound) + RAF RBD — disrupted interaction

comparative_analysis.conclusion: MRTX1133 binding reduces KRAS-RAF interface contacts by 60% and weakens binding by ~6300-fold. The drug-bound Switch I conformation displaces key interface residues (I36, E37, D38, Y40, R41, K42), directly blocking the RAF RBD binding surface.

summary.verdict: PRODIGY confirms the paper's claim that MRTX1133 disrupts effector binding. KRAS-RAF interaction weakens from KD ~1 nM (apo) to KD ~6.2 μM (drug-bound) — a >6000-fold reduction. This is mechanistically consistent with the paper's reported IC50 of 9 nM for blocking RAF RBD interaction, as the conformational change induced by MRTX1133 eliminates most Switch I interface contacts.
